title: Zealandia Wildlife Sanctuary
meta_desc: Discover Zealandia Wildlife Sanctuary in Wellington, a predator-free haven for native NZ species. Perfect for families, with prices from $11/child and $22/adult. Learn about the best times to visit and how to save money.
intro: Zealandia Wildlife Sanctuary is a must-visit attraction for families in Wellington, offering a unique opportunity to spot native NZ species in a predator-free environment. With its stunning natural beauty and abundance of wildlife, Zealandia is an ideal destination for families with kids of all ages. The sanctuary is open daily, with day visits and night tours available, catering to different interests and age groups.

sections.heading: What to Expect
sections.body:
Zealandia Wildlife Sanctuary is a 225-hectare valley that is home to over 40 native species, including tuatara, kākā, little blue penguins, and kiwi. The sanctuary offers a range of walking tracks, from easy to challenging, that allow visitors to explore the valley and spot wildlife freely. The day visit is suitable for families with kids of all ages, from 0+, and takes around 2-3 hours to complete.

The night tour, which is a separate booking, is an excellent way to spot kiwi and other nocturnal species. The tour takes around 2 hours and is led by experienced guides who provide insight into the sanctuary's unique ecosystem.

sections.heading: Value for Money and Insider Tips
sections.body:
Zealandia Wildlife Sanctuary offers great value for money, with prices starting from $11/child and $22/adult. To make the most of your visit, it's essential to plan ahead and book in advance, especially during peak season. Consider visiting early in the morning or later in the afternoon to avoid the crowds and make the most of your time.

Additionally, be sure to wear comfortable shoes and bring plenty of water and snacks, as the walking tracks can be challenging and the sanctuary is located in a valley.
list: Book in advance to avoid crowds

sections.heading: Best Time to Visit and Booking
sections.body:
The best time to visit Zealandia Wildlife Sanctuary is during the spring and summer months, when the weather is warm and the wildlife is more active. However, the sanctuary is open year-round, and each season offers a unique experience. To book your visit, simply go to the Zealandia website and follow the online booking process.

Be sure to check for any special deals or discounts, especially for families and groups. Zealandia also offers a range of membership options, which can provide significant savings for frequent visitors.
list: Spring and summer: best time to visit
Check for special deals and discounts

sections.heading: Downsides for Families
sections.body:
While Zealandia Wildlife Sanctuary is an excellent destination for families, there are a few downsides to consider. The sanctuary is located in a valley, which can make it challenging for strollers and wheelchairs. Additionally, some of the walking tracks can be steep and uneven, which may not be suitable for very young children or those with mobility issues.

However, the sanctuary offers a range of amenities, including a café and toilets, and the staff are always happy to provide assistance and advice to visitors.
list: Challenging terrain for strollers and wheelchairs
Some tracks may not be suitable for young children
